title: Psychotria capitellata
intro: Psychotria capitellata, also known as Uva clausa, is a species of plant in the Rubiaceae family native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as being used to treat fever, gastrointestinal issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria capitellata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Psychotria capitellata is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as the coffee family or madder family. The Rubiaceae family includes around 650 genera and over 13,000 species, many of which are native to tropical regions. The plants within this family have a range of growth habits, from shrubs to small trees. Psychotria capitellata is classified as a member of this large and complex family due to its shared characteristics and morphological features.
